title: FMT1 modifier stripping in verify is spec-correct with clear explanation
description: EccKey::verify strips TPM2 FMT1 modifier bits (P-flag bit 8, S-flag bit 6, subject number bits 11:9 = mask 0x0F40) before comparing to TPM_RC_SIGNATURE. The condition (rc & 0x80) \!= 0 && (rc & 0xF000) == 0 is the correct discriminator for FMT1 codes per TPM2 Part 2 §6.6.3. The comment explains why 'bit 8 clear' is not used as the discriminator (subject number 1 sets bit 8, so TPM_RC_SIGNATURE+param_1 = 0x1DB would bypass stripping). A future contributor might suggest simplifying this to a direct rc == TPM_RC_SIGNATURE check; this would break for any FMT1-decorated return code from wolfTPM.
